title: The Wonderful Wizard of Oz
summary: Dorothy lives in a small house in Kansas with her family suddenly.
coverScene: A little girl in a blue and white dress stands in the doorway of a small house, looking out at a vast gray prairie with a few trees in the distance, and a small black dog by her side.

sentences.text: Dorothy lived in the middle of the great flat land of Kansas.
She lived there with her Uncle Henry and her Aunt Em.
Their house was very small.
The wood of the house had been burned gray by the hot sun.
There was a small wooden step in front of the door.
Under the house, there was a small hole in the ground.
This hole was a safe room under the ground.
The family could go there if a very strong wind storm came.
If a storm had come, they would have run there immediately.
Uncle Henry was a farmer who worked hard from morning until night.
He never laughed and he looked very serious.
Aunt Em was also very thin and she did not smile anymore.
When she first came to this flat land, she was a young and happy wife.
However, the wind and the sun had taken all the color from her face.
Now she was gray and quiet, just like the land around her.
Dorothy was a little girl who brought life back to the house.
She had a little black dog named Toto.
Toto had long, soft hair and small black eyes that shone with joy.
Dorothy played with him all day and she loved him very much.
He was the one who made her laugh when everything else was gray.
One day, Uncle Henry stood on the step and looked at the sky.
The sky was turning a dark and ugly gray.
He could hear the low sound of the wind from the south.
He knew that a dangerous wind storm was coming.
He went to look after the animals on the farm.
Aunt Em came to the door and saw the danger too.
She was frightened by the dark clouds.
She called out to Dorothy, telling her to run to the safe room under the ground.
The wind began to blow very hard.
It shook the small house.
Dorothy ran inside to get her dog.
Toto had jumped under the bed because he was afraid of the noise.
Dorothy had to find him before she could run to safety.
Aunt Em had already gone down into the room under the ground.
She called for Dorothy to follow her.
But Dorothy was still inside, holding Toto in her arms.
Before she could walk to the door, a strange thing happened.
The wind hit the house with great force.
The house shook so hard that Dorothy fell on the floor.
Then, the house was lifted up into the air.
It rose slowly, like a big bag of gas rising into the sky.
It was carried by the wind to the very top of the storm.
The house was held by the wind for a long time.
It was moved miles and miles away from Kansas.
At first, Dorothy was very scared.
She thought the house would fall and be destroyed.
She felt her head spin as the house turned around and around.
The spinning made her feel quite sick.
She lay down on her bed and held Toto tight.
The dog did not like the movement and he ran around the room.
He made a lot of noise, but Dorothy remained quiet.
After some time, she realized that they were not falling.
The house was being carried gently, as if it were a boat on a quiet river.
If she had looked out the window, she would have seen only dark clouds.
She decided to close her eyes and wait.
Eventually, she fell asleep, despite the noise of the wind outside.
Dorothy slept for many hours.
When she finally woke up, she noticed that the house was no longer moving.
There was no wind, and the air was very quiet.
Suddenly, the house landed with a big crash.
The shake was so strong that Dorothy was thrown onto the floor.
However, she was not hurt.
She sat up and listened carefully.
She could hear the sweet sound of birds singing outside.
She also saw bright sunlight coming through the window.
She went to the door and opened it.
Toto followed her closely, his tail moving with excitement.
She looked out and was very surprised by the sight.
She was no longer in the gray land of Kansas.
Instead, she was in a beautiful country that was full of life.
There were tall green trees with sweet fruits.
The ground was covered with grass and flowers of many colors.
Bright yellow, red, and blue flowers were growing everywhere.
The sun was shining with bright light, and the sky was a clear blue.
This was a place that she had never seen before.
It was a wonderful change from the dry, gray fields of her home.
If her aunt had been there, she would have been amazed by the beauty.
As she stood there, she saw some people coming toward her.
They were small people, and they wore strange clothes.
They looked at Dorothy with great respect.
Then, she noticed something under her house.
Two feet, wearing silver shoes, were sticking out from under the wood.
The people explained that her house had landed on top of an evil magic woman.
This woman had ruled their land with fear for many years.
Now, because of the house, the evil magic woman was dead.
The people were very happy to be free from her power.
They thanked Dorothy for saving them from this terrible person.
Dorothy was surprised to hear this.
She had not intended to kill anyone.
She explained that it was an accident caused by the storm.
However, the people still praised her.
They offered her food and beautiful gifts.
Dorothy thanked them, but she felt a bit sad.
She thought about her Uncle Henry and her Aunt Em.
They would be worrying about her.
She knew she must find a way to get back to Kansas.
If she stayed here, her family would think she was dead.
She asked the people if they knew the way to her home.
The people shook their heads.
They told her that Kansas was a very far place.
It was separated from their land by a huge desert.
No one had ever crossed that desert and survived.
They told her about another powerful person who lived in a great city.
He was a good magic man who might be able to help her.
If anyone could help her, it would be him.
Dorothy decided that she must travel to this city to ask for his assistance.
She prepared for her long journey with Toto by her side.
She hoped she would see her family again soon.

quiz.q: What was the name of Dorothy's little black dog?
Where did Dorothy live with her Uncle Henry and Aunt Em?
What happened to the house during the big storm?
options: It was carried away by the wind

sentences.text: Dorothy lived with her Uncle Henry and Aunt Em.
Their house was small.
It was on the great land of Kansas.
The house was gray.
It had a low roof.
There was a little porch in front of the house.
Inside, the house was also small.
There was a small hole in the ground outside.
This hole was a storm cellar.
The family would go into this cellar.
They went there if a very big storm came.
This kept them safe from bad weather.
Uncle Henry was a farmer.
He worked very hard.
He worked from morning until night.
Aunt Em was a kind woman.
But she had become thin.
She had also become very serious.
This was because she had lived on the land for many years.
Life on the land was hard.
Dorothy loved to play with her little black dog.
His name was Toto.
Toto was a small dog.
He made Dorothy laugh every day.
He was her best friend.
One day, Uncle Henry looked at the sky.
He saw a big storm coming.
The sky was dark and gray.
The wind was blowing very hard.
It made a loud noise.
Aunt Em felt afraid.
She knew the storm was dangerous.
She told Dorothy to go to the storm cellar.
It was important to go there fast.
But they could not get to the cellar in time.
The storm got much worse.
The house started to shake a lot.
Strong winds lifted the house up.
It rose into the air.
The wind carried it far away.
Dorothy was very scared.
She held on to Toto hard.
She waited to see what would happen.
She did not know what to do.
The house was carried high up.
It went very far into the air.
Dorothy felt like she was going up in a balloon.
This was a strange feeling.
She looked out the window.
She saw the ground far below her.
Everything looked very small.
The house was spinning around and around.
Dorothy got dizzy from the movement.
She felt sick.
After a while, the house started to fall.
Dorothy was scared again.
She thought it would crash with great force.
But it did not fall to the ground.
Instead, it landed with a big crash.
The crash made a loud sound.
Dorothy was shaken.
But she was okay.
She got out of the house slowly.
She wanted to see where she was now.
She looked around carefully.
She found herself in a beautiful land.
It was very different from Kansas.
There were green trees everywhere.
Colorful flowers grew all around.
Many birds were singing sweet songs.
A bright sun was shining in the sky.
Dorothy was very happy to be alive.
She was happy to see such a beautiful place.
It felt like a dream.
She looked around again.
She saw that the house had landed on a witch.
The witch was dead now.
The people of this new land were very happy.
They had waited a long time for this.
They came to Dorothy.
They thanked her for killing the bad witch.
They asked her to stay with them for a while.
They said she was a hero.
"If you stay with us," they told her, "we will be very happy."
Dorothy was happy to stay for a short time.
But she also wanted to go home.
She has missed her Uncle Henry very much.
She has missed Aunt Em a lot.
She wanted to see them again soon.
She felt a little sad.
She asked the people of the land for help.
She wanted to find a way home to Kansas.
She did not know the way.

quiz.q: What did Uncle Henry see coming in the sky?
Why did Aunt Em tell Dorothy to go to the cellar?
options: Because it was time for dinner.
Because it was a safe place from the storm.
Because she wanted to play a game.

quiz.q: What did Dorothy find when she got out of the house in the new land?
options: Her Uncle Henry and Aunt Em.
A bad witch who was alive.
A dead witch under her house.
